topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a grating soundtrack to my mounting frustration. I watched Sarah receive the congratulatory email, a subtle, knowing smile playing on her lips as she accepted the acknowledgment for the Senior Manager role. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles in my neck strain. I reread the internal memo, the details of the criteria for the promotion, and the list of candidates. My name wasn't there. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor, and walked towards the breakroom, needing air that didn't taste of stale coffee and unfairness. "This is just absurd," I muttered to the empty room, my voice tight with suppressed energy. Every glance directed toward Sarah felt like a physical jab.

[story 2]

I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles turned white. The air in the conference room felt thick, suffocating. When Mark was announced as the chosen successor, a slow, burning sensation started low in my stomach and spread outward. I forced a polite nod, the movement feeling stiff and unnatural. Later, as I packed my bag, I couldn't shake the feeling of being deliberately overlooked. I paced the hallway, my footsteps echoing too loudly in the quiet corridor. "They just don't see the work I've done," I whispered, the words sounding hollow even to my own ears. I stopped by the window, staring out at the city lights, feeling a hot flush creep up my neck.

[story 3]

When the announcement came, the sound of applause felt distant, muffled. I watched Sarah’s triumphant expression, a picture of deserved success, and a sharp, unpleasant clenching occurred in my chest. I stood motionless, my hands clenched into fists inside my pockets. "It's a complete oversight," I managed to say, the words coming out sharper than intended, laced with a brittle edge. I turned away quickly, needing distance from the scene. Walking back to my cubicle, I slammed my laptop shut with unnecessary force. The silence of my workspace amplified the throbbing behind my eyes; I needed to organize something, anything, just to redirect this intense, unwelcome feeling.
